commit.message:
Rename package from com.notification to org.notification

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Grafana dashboard by setting datasource UID and enabling histogram metrics

Dashboard panels referenced datasource uid "prometheus" but the
provisioned datasource had no explicit UID. Also enabled percentiles
histogram for HTTP server requests so p95/p99 latency panels have data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Redis cache serialization by using JSON instead of JDK serializer

The default JdkSerializationRedisSerializer requires cached objects to
implement Serializable. Entity classes (User, Device, NotificationTemplate)
don't, causing every @Cacheable call to fail with SerializationException
and resulting in 100% load test failures. Switch to
GenericJackson2JsonRedisSerializer. Also promote load-client error
logging from DEBUG to ERROR for better visibility.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace wildcard imports with explicit single-class imports

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add healthcheck to notification-server for load-client readiness

The load-client was starting before the notification-server Spring Boot
app was ready, causing 100% request failures. Use actuator health
endpoint as healthcheck and require service_healthy condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Docker build by generating per-service settings.gradle

Each Dockerfile was copying the shared settings.gradle which references
all subprojects, but only one subproject's source was available during
build. Generate a minimal settings.gradle with only the target subproject.

feat: сповіщення про угоди, trailing stop ATR, статус Telegram, виправлення таймауту

- Сповіщення в Telegram після кожного TP/SL з PnL% (з урахуванням плеча)
- TP3=0 → TRAILING_STOP_MARKET на Binance з callbackRate = 1.5×ATR/ціна×100
- /status та /balance: повні дані рахунку, вхід/ціна/%, ліквідація, SL/TP рівні
- Таймаут моніторингу: 24h на кожен TP (скидається після кожного) замість 4h загальних
- Виправлення -2021 при встановленні TP/trail SL: ринкове закриття як fallback
- Очищення залишкових ордерів при закритті позиції
- README переписано українською з новими функціями

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: atomic TP renew - PLACE first, skip CANCEL if blocked (PR-P0-TP-RENEW-ATOMIC) (#367)

Prevents TP loss during renew when gates (MAX_POSITION, FSM) block the
new PLACE. Old order: [CANCEL, PLACE] - CANCEL always passed, PLACE
could be blocked, leaving position without TP protection.

New order: [PLACE, CANCEL] - engine tracks PLACE result per symbol;
if blocked, CANCEL is skipped with TP_RENEW_PLACE_FAILED reason and
old TP stays alive. Also fixes dead _tp_renew_attempts counter.

Co-authored-by: bnzr-hub <256895926+bnzr-hub@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(browser): programs browser window on no-app launch (#24)

* feat(browser): add programs browser window for no-app launch (#7)

Opens a native directory viewer listing !App directories from
assets/programs/ when the emulator starts without a CLI program
argument. Each app is shown as a sprite icon (extracted from !Sprites)
plus its display name; double-click launches the app via !Run.
Drag-and-drop of a !App directory installs it, runs !Boot, and
refreshes the view.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: load and run ARM application binaries (AIF support)

Add Machine.startApp() to load ARM binaries into application space after
ROM boot, with MEMC forceMapRange() to ensure the app slot is fully mapped
and minimal exception vectors installed. Extend MEMC logical-to-physical
translation to handle addresses above the 512 KB window for AIF decompression.

Add RISC OS module registry with built-in stubs so RMLoad/RMEnsure SWIs
succeed for common ROM modules (SharedCLibrary, Wimp, FontManager, etc.).
Add SWI trace logging on the CPU for debugging unhandled calls. Various
updates to the Obey interpreter, SWI dispatcher, Wimp manager, and
Electron shell to support launching apps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: introduce Logger class in shared; address PR review comments

Add Logger to @theprogramminggiantpanda/shared with debug/info/error
levels and a configurable LogLevel. Pass a Logger instance from the
Electron shell (debug in dev, error in production) through to
ArchimedesMachine and ARM2CPU, replacing all scattered console.*/
process.stdout.write calls with logger.debug()/error() calls.

Remove the swiTraceEnabled flag from ARM2CPU — SWI trace visibility is
now controlled entirely by the logger level. Use IPC constants from
shared in programs-browser-preload instead of hardcoded channel strings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Replace splitArg/split(",") patterns with commander's native argParser
for repeatable options. This is the idiomatic commander.js approach:

add @me support to pr count command's assignee option.

Note: -p/--project with .env("BACKLOG_PROJECT") retains comma-separated
parsing since env vars provide a single string value.

commit.message:
fix: catalogue installer fails clearly when package is empty or all imports fail

Previously an empty catalogue (no manifest.json files found) returned
success=true and still registered the package as installed, making it
appear as if primitives were imported when none were.

- Empty catalogue now returns success=false with a clear message explaining
  where manifest.json files are expected
- DB registration is now conditional: only records the package when at
  least one primitive was imported or already existed
- All-failed imports also return success=false

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): make End button more visible on mobile

Upgrade from plain text link to pill button with background,
red hover state, and uppercase tracking for better tap target.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(backend): account 404 for high-storage addresses (e.g. 0x82ed1b9cba5bb1b3)

Flow access nodes reject GetAccount for accounts exceeding 20MB storage limit.
The fallback path used COUNT(*) on address_transactions which took 205s for
high-activity addresses (12M+ rows), exceeding the HTTP context deadline and
causing the fallback to return nil → 404.

- Replace COUNT(*) with EXISTS for instant existence check
- Remove nil return from buildAccountFallback so RPC errors always get a
  degraded response instead of 404
- Enrich fallback with contract names from DB

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Replace per-app PORT variables with unified EXIT_PORT

Each app had a unique APPID_PORT variable (e.g. WHOAMI_PORT, IMMICH_PORT) in
both .toml defaults and .yml compose templates. This was redundant since ports
are allocated dynamically. Now all apps use a single EXIT_PORT variable injected
by code, eliminating the toml defaults entirely.

Also makes HealthConfig.container_port optional for apps like Beszel where the
container listens on the dynamic host port.

feat: add OAuth2 Device Code flow for Outlook/Hotmail/Live accounts

Microsoft deprecated basic auth for consumer IMAP/SMTP. This adds:
- OAuth2 Device Code Grant (RFC 8628) for CLI-based authentication
- SASL XOAUTH2 for IMAP (ImapFlow) and SMTP (Nodemailer)
- Persistent token storage (~/.better-email-mcp/tokens.json)
- Automatic token refresh with 5-minute buffer
- Auto-detection of Outlook domains (outlook.com, hotmail.com, live.com)
- CLI: `npx @n24q02m/better-email-mcp auth user@outlook.com`

Uses outlook.office.com resource URL (personal MSA accounts).
Tested with real Outlook account: IMAP (229 messages) + SMTP verified.

commit.message:
feat(color-contrast-cli): add actionable error messages (#91)

* feat(color-contrast-cli): add actionable error messages with format-specific hints

Color parsing errors now analyze the input to detect the intended format
and provide specific hints (e.g., invalid hex characters, missing %,
comma usage in space-only formats, bare hex without #).

CLI argument errors now give context-specific messages for 0, 1, or 3+
arguments instead of a generic help hint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(color-contrast-cli): use actual function name in rgba()/hsla() diagnostics

Error messages now reflect the function name the user typed (e.g.,
"rgba() requires 3 color channels" instead of "rgb() requires ...").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(color-contrast-cli): remove unused format parameter from diagnoseSpaceOnly

The format parameter became unused after switching to funcName extracted
from the input string.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(color-contrast-cli): add tests for uncovered diagnostic fallback paths

Cover the "could not parse" fallback paths for rgb(), hsl(), and hwb()
when tokens look structurally correct but contain invalid values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(color-contrast-cli): strip alpha from comma-separated tokens in diagnostics

splitTokens now removes the optional 4th value (alpha) for comma syntax,
matching the alpha stripping already done for space syntax. This prevents
misdiagnosing rgba(R, G, B, invalid)/hsla(H, S, L, invalid) as wrong
channel count when the real issue is an invalid alpha value.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add FilterPipeline for PII and sensitive data redaction (#16)

* feat: add FilterPipeline for PII and sensitive data redaction (#16)

Implement FilterPipeline class with:
- PII detection via presidio-analyzer (emails, phone, SSN, etc.)
- Secret detection via regex patterns (API keys, tokens, credentials)
- Per-user configurable via filter_pii and filter_sensitive flags
- Redaction tokens: [REDACTED:PII:ENTITY_TYPE], [REDACTED:SECRET]
- Filter event tracking: filter_triggered, filter_type, before/after snippets

Add 17 comprehensive unit tests with 92.65% coverage.
Update pyproject.toml with presidio, detect-secrets, spacy dependencies.
Export FilterPipeline and FilterResult from mcp_hub module.

* ci: download en_core_web_sm spaCy model before running presidio tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: use uv pip to install spaCy model — poetry venv has no pip binary

The uv-managed venv doesn't include pip, so `spacy download` (which
shells out to pip) fails with "No module named pip".  Use `uv pip
install` with the direct wheel URL instead.

* fix(test): mock AnalyzerEngine in FilterPipeline tests — prevents spaCy model download in CI

feat: transmit BNO085 pitch/roll via UART and display in telemetry

(millidegrees, same ×1000 scaling as mg) for BNO085 frames.

web UI uses sensor-fusion values directly instead of deriving
pitch/roll from a zeroed accelerometer vector.
Also removes unused diag_bmi160.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix FA dom.watch() SVG orphan bug in toggle buttons

FA's dom.watch() replaces <i> tags with <svg>, breaking Svelte's
conditional rendering (orphaned SVGs on state change). Fix: wrap each
branch in <span class="btn-content" style="display:contents"> so Svelte
removes the wrapper and its entire subtree cleanly. Also adds CSS-only
.woof-spinner to avoid FA spinner icons in toggle buttons.

Other fixes:
- ParkActionBar props mismatch (was passing non-existent scrollToCheckin)
- getMyDogs infinite loop ($effect re-triggering on empty array)
- Follow "Already following" error now gracefully syncs state
- Schedule visit stays inline only (not in floating bar)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Classify TypedParamPort → §6.20.1 [skip ci]

The test exercises `parameter_port_declaration` with an explicit data_type — syntax like `parameter int W = 8, int D = 4`. The formal grammar for `parameter_port_declaration`, including the `data_type list_of_param_assignments` alternative (the bare typed form used for subsequent parameters sharing a type within a port list), is defined in §6.20.1 (Syntax 6-6, "Parameter declaration syntax"). §23.2.1 introduces the `parameter_port_list` in the module header syntax but delegates the `parameter_port_declaration` production to §6.20.1, making §6.20.1 the more specific governing subclause.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign UI, fix bugs, add mobile support, and add full documentation

UI & UX:
- Rewrite CSS to monochrome design with CSS custom properties and BEM naming
- Switch article list from card grid to compact table layout
- Add full mobile responsiveness (stacked cards <480px, grid 480–767px, table ≥768px)
- Add Roboto + Roboto Mono fonts via Google Fonts
- Replace default favicon with custom black RC monogram SVG

Bug fixes & performance:
- Fix duplicate seed data by rewriting seeds.rb to use find_or_create_by!
- Fix badge CSS class mismatch (badge-published → badge--published) in show view
- Add favicon route redirect to silence 404 on /favicon.ico
- Add .load to index action to prevent N+1 queries (4 SQL → 1)
- Pin minitest to ~> 5.25 for Rails 8.1 compatibility
- Fix stale CSS selector in controller tests (.article-card → .article-row)
- Fix system test button labels to match new UI

Database:
- Add migration to enforce published NOT NULL, default false, and add indexes

Cleanup:
- Move unused files (Tailwind CSS, PWA manifests, mailer layouts, SQLite DLLs) to trash/

Documentation (all new):
- docs/WINDOWS_GUIDE.md — Windows setup, PATH fix, daily workflow
- docs/LEARNING_ROADMAP.md — 14 features to build next, ordered by difficulty
- docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md — Annotated reference for every folder and file
- docs/TUTORIAL.md — 10-part step-by-step Rails learning walkthrough
- docs/NEW_MODULE_GUIDE.md — How to build a new CRUD module from scratch
- README.md — Complete rewrite with accurate stack info, API examples, and doc links
